chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No. D- 5645 of 2025 along with C.P No. D- 4373 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For order on office objection. 2. For hearing of CMA No. 23628/25. 3. For hearing of main case. 03.02.2026 Mr. Ali Zaidi, Advocate for Petitioner in CP No. D- 5645/2025 and for the Respondent No.6 in CP No. D- 4373/2025. Mr. Naveed Ahmed, Advocate holding brief for Mr. Salahuddin Ahmed, Advocate for Petitioner in CP No. D- 4373/2025. Mr. Ilyas Khan Tanoli, Advocate for the Applicant in CP No. D- 4373/2025. Mr. Farmanullah Khan, Advocate for Respondent No.3. Mr. Junaid Niazi, Advocate for Respondent No.5 in CP No. D- 4373/2025. Mr. Kalash A. Vaswani, AAG. Mr. Muhammad Akbar Khan, Assistant Attorney General. ---------- Learned counsel in attendance jointly state that the operation of the Petrol Pump that is the subject matter of these Petitions has ceased in view of the Order dated 27.11.2025, pending the process of issuance of Permits/Licenses, as sought by the Petitioner in C.P No. D- 5645/2025, which remains under way. Counsel appearing on behalf of the said Petitioner undertakes that no such operations would be carried out in the absence of the relevant Permits/Licenses, but seeks that the Applications pending in that regard with OGRA and the Explosives Department be decided expeditiously. Under the circumstances, it appears that the purpose of these Petitions stands served. Accordingly, the same are disposed of, leaving the Petitioners to pursue its applications before OGRA as well as Explosive Department, which ought to be decided without undue delay preferably within a period of 45 days from today.

Under the circumstances, it appears that the purpose of these Petitions stands served. Accordingly, the same are disposed of, leaving the Petitioners to pursue its applications before OGRA as well as Explosive Department, which ought to be decided without undue delay preferably within a period of 45 days from today. Office to place copy of this order in the connected petition. Judge Judge Ayaz
